best_extraction.authors: Al-Kaisy A, Van Buyten JP, Carganillo R, Caraway D, Gliner B, Subbaroyan J, Panwar C, Rotte A, Amirdelfan K, Kapural L
best_extraction.title: 10 kHz SCS therapy for chronic pain, effects on opioid usage: Post hoc analysis of data from two prospective studies
best_extraction.study_question: Does 10 kHz SCS reduce opioid use and pain in chronic back and leg pain patients?
best_extraction.population_assessed: Adults with chronic low back and/or leg pain receiving 10 kHz SCS, mainly failed back surgery syndrome.
best_extraction.outcome_measures: Opioid dose MME/day, back pain VAS, leg pain VAS
best_extraction.inclusion_criteria: Adults with chronic back and/or leg pain, VAS at least 5 cm, SCS candidates.
best_extraction.answer_to_study_question: 10 kHz SCS reduced opioid dose and high-risk opioid use while significantly improving back and leg pain.

reasoning: Most fields showed unanimous agreement. For study design and sample size, Anthropic and OpenRouter aligned; Google differed despite lower design confidence and likely counted completers.

field_level_decisions.study_design: Chose prospective_cohort_controlled from two models; article is a post hoc analysis of prospective studies, not a standalone trial.
field_level_decisions.number_of_participants_in_study: Used 137 from two models; 129 likely reflects a subset or follow-up sample.
field_level_decisions.volume_issue_pages: Preferred concise consensus format 9:11441 over expanded pagination.
field_level_decisions.population_assessed: Merged shared description with failed back surgery syndrome detail from higher-confidence Anthropic output.
